title: .bbm.bld sidecar
description: Fork-only metadata for a BlueBrick .bbm map: cross-layer modules, anchored text labels, and event-venue geometry. Lives next to the .bbm so vanilla BlueBrick 1.9.2 can still open the .bbm cleanly without needing any fork awareness.

schemaVersion.description: Sidecar schema version. 1 is the only version shipped so far. Readers should reject versions newer than they understand.

bbmHashSha256.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the sibling .bbm file's bytes at the time this sidecar was written. On load, readers compare against the current .bbm hash — a mismatch means the .bbm was edited externally (e.g. in vanilla BlueBrick), and anchored-label targets / module members may need re-linking. Empty string = hash not computed.

anchoredLabels.description: Text labels whose position sticks to a target item (brick / group / module) when the target moves or rotates. On .bbm save, also written flattened to world-coordinate text cells so vanilla still renders them in the right place at snapshot time.

modules.description: Cross-layer groups — bundles items across multiple layers into a named unit that moves or rotates together. A module's per-layer subset is also written as a vanilla same-layer Group inside the .bbm so vanilla sees reasonable grouping within each affected layer.

venue.description: Optional physical event space the layout targets: outline, edge classifications (wall / door / open), interior obstacles, walkway buffer. A project has at most one venue. Vanilla BlueBrick has no Venue concept; the .bbm.bld is the only place this lives.

color.description: Color with optional KnownColor preservation for round-trip.

known.description: True iff this color came from a .NET KnownColor enum rather than free-form ARGB.

name.description: KnownColor enum name (e.g. 'Red', 'Black'). Present iff known=true.

font.description: Font spec round-tripped as .NET System.Drawing.Font triple.

kind.description: AnchorKind — 0=World (absolute world-stud offset), 1=Brick (offset is in target-brick local coords; moves with the brick), 2=Group (attached to an upstream same-layer Group), 3=Module (attached to a cross-layer Module).

targetId.description: guid of the anchor target. Meaning depends on `kind`: brick.guid for Brick, group.guid for Group, module.id for Module, ignored (empty) for World.

offset.description: Offset from the anchor, in studs. Interpretation depends on `kind` (world coords for World, local coords for everything else).

rot.description: Rotation of the label relative to the target, in degrees.

minZoom.description: Optional visibility cutoff: label hidden at zooms below this. 0 = always visible.

members.description: guids of every item in the module, across any layer. Can include brick / text / ruler guids.

transform.description: 3×3 QTransform matrix in row-major order (m11 m12 m13 m21 m22 m23 m31 m32 m33). Identity is [1,0,0,0,1,0,0,0,1].

sourceFile.description: Path of the .bbm this module was imported from, when applicable. Relative or absolute is caller-dependent.

importedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the import. Absent when the module was created from selection rather than imported.

enabled.description: Allows the user to toggle the venue off without deleting it.

minWalkwayStuds.description: Minimum walkway clearance from the inside of Door / Open edges. In studs (converted to/from mm per BlueBrick convention: 1 stud = 8 mm).

bounds.description: Optional explicit reserved layout footprint. When empty, the footprint is inferred from `edges`.

edges.description: Closed loop of segments making up the venue perimeter. Each edge is polyline + kind. Order matters (adjacent edges share endpoints).

obstacles.description: Interior polygons (pillars, columns, fixed furniture) that bricks mustn't overlap.

kind.description: EdgeKind — 0=Wall (solid barrier, no walkway buffer needed), 1=Door (walkway clearance required; `doorWidthStuds` > 0), 2=Open (no barrier, indicates viewing side; walkway buffer applies).

doorWidthStuds.description: Door clear width in studs. Only meaningful when `kind == 1` (Door).

poly.description: Segment polyline in world studs. Most edges are two points; curved doors can use more.
